name: Inland Fair Housing and Mediation Board
description_short: Private nonprofit offering free fair housing services, landlord-tenant mediation, housing counseling, and senior services across inland Southern California.
description_long:
Inland Fair Housing and Mediation Board (IFHMB) is a private nonprofit agency serving communities in inland Southern California, including San Bernardino County and the Coachella Valley. Founded to address housing justice and community stability, the organization operates as a multi-service provider focused on vulnerable populations. The agency maintains physical offices but adapted operations during closures while continuing service delivery via phone and email.

IFHMB provides fair housing discrimination reporting and advocacy, landlord-tenant mediation to resolve disputes without court proceedings, and free housing counseling specializing in foreclosure prevention. Their housing counseling covers loan modifications, special forbearances, loan repayment plans, and loan reinstatement plans—all at no cost. The organization also offers Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP) application support and reverse mortgage counseling. Senior Services (age 55+) are available to Ontario residents and include HEAP assistance and referrals for affordable senior housing across six San Bernardino County cities.

IFHMB distinguishes itself through geographic specificity and population focus rather than broad national reach. They maintain extensive local referral networks for seniors and operate dedicated hotlines for specific communities (Indio: 1-888-885-8020; El Centro: 1-888-745-9330). The organization provides accessibility accommodations under Section 504 and demonstrates commitment to underserved populations through free counseling and mediation services.

As a nonprofit, IFHMB offers genuine free services without financial product sales pressure. However, the organization's reach is limited to specific geographic areas—senior services specifically serve Ontario residents only—and phone-based contact is required during temporary office closures. Consumers should verify service availability for their location before contacting.
pros: All housing counseling services are completely free, including foreclosure prevention and loan modification guidance
Specialized fair housing discrimination reporting and advocacy with dedicated hotline (909-984-2254 ext. 175 or 1-800-321-0911)
Free landlord-tenant mediation services to resolve disputes without court involvement
Extensive local senior services program with referrals for affordable housing across six San Bernardino County cities
Multiple contact methods including phone lines, email, and community-specific hotlines (Indio and El Centro)
Section 504 accessibility accommodations and designated Section 504 Coordinator available
HUD-affiliated housing counseling services covering foreclosure prevention, reverse mortgages, and loan restructuring
cons: Senior Services limited to residents age 55+ in City of Ontario only, excluding most of service area
Temporary office closure to public limits in-person access; services require phone/email contact
Geographic service limitations—primarily inland Southern California counties; not available nationwide
Website contains broken/incomplete information (office closure end date shows '202 7' instead of complete year)
Limited scope for some services—reverse mortgage counseling mentioned but details sparse
best_for: Homeowners facing foreclosure in San Bernardino County or Coachella Valley seeking free prevention counseling
Tenants and landlords in dispute who need affordable mediation services
Renters reporting housing discrimination or fair housing violations in inland Southern California
Senior citizens age 55+ in Ontario, CA seeking HEAP assistance and affordable senior housing referrals
diagnosis: IFHMB is best for inland Southern California residents seeking free, nonprofit housing counseling, fair housing advocacy, or mediation services. The main caveat is strict geographic limitations—senior services exclusively serve Ontario residents, and the organization operates only in specific counties, making it unavailable for consumers outside their service areas.
services: Fair housing discrimination reporting and advocacy
Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP) application support
Senior services and referrals (age 55+, Ontario residents)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3175 Sedona Ct Ste D, Suite 2, Ontario, CA 91764
